bead.title: server: forward bead lifecycle mutations to owner with audit metadata
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Lifecycle bead mutations (approve/block/cancel/reopen) invoked from the hub web UI against a spoke-owned project are not forwarded to the owning node, and forwarded writes do not carry origin/forwarding audit metadata, so lifecycle events on federated projects are lost or untraceable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:12 returns a bead store rooted only at the per-request working directory, so lifecycle resolvers built on it cannot reach a spoke owner.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:112 (and the lifecycle resolvers that share this local-store path) mutate locally without emitting forwarding/origin audit metadata.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:353 requires owner-targeted, project-scoped bead queue mutations in hub-mode.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340 (TC-018) covers cross-project lifecycle mutations and federated writes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route beadApprove, beadBlock, beadCancel, beadReopen (and any dependency mutations exposed on this resolver) through local project context or federation owner forwarding, reusing the owner-resolution path established for create/update.
- Preserve existing lifecycle events on the owning node for forwarded writes and attach origin/forwarding audit metadata.
- Return typed errors for missing required reasons/notes, non-owned targets, and offline/read_only spokes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new lifecycle states or dependency-edit UI beyond already exposed mutation surfaces.
- Create/update forwarding and project isolation are covered by the sibling create/update bead.
- No Playwright/UI wiring in this bead.

Governing artifacts: FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md:353, TP-002-server-web-ui.md:340 (TC-018). Depends on the create/update owner-forwarding bead for the shared owner-resolution path.
bead.acceptance:
1. Go test `TestFederatedBeadLifecycle_ForwardsAuditMetadata` proves approve/block/cancel/reopen for a spoke project are forwarded to the owner, preserve the lifecycle events on the owning node, and carry origin/forwarding audit metadata returned in a hub federated read.
2. Go test `TestBeadLifecycle_RequiredReasonTypedErrors` proves typed errors are returned when required reasons/notes are missing, for non-owned targets, and for offline/read_only spokes.
3. `cd cli && go test -short ./internal/server/graphql/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/federation/...` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
